creator: linus@nordberg.se
text:
| 1.2 +0 -11 ports/security/heimdal/files/extrapatch-lib_hdb_hdb-ldap.c (dead)

This patch to lib/hdb/hdb-ldap.c adding a default path to the LDAP
socket has simply been commented out from the Makefile.  Is there a
reason for removing this?


| 1.2 +0 -21 ports/security/heimdal/files/kpasswdd-cracklib.c.in (dead)

I don't think that the kpasswdd-cracklib.c.in file should have been
nuked.  It's still needed when -DWITH_CRACKLIB.


| 1.3 +0 -17 ports/security/heimdal/files/patch-ch (dead)

I'm not sure about this patch to ltmain.sh, which disables the "Install
the pseudo-library for information purposes" block.  Motivation for
removing it?

-- 
Linus
